messages.content: lol failed another bio test today, my parents are gonna actually end me when they see my report card

Ugh, that sucks! Bio tests can be brutal. What was the test on? Sometimes it just takes finding the right way to study for that kind of material - like some people do way better with diagrams or flashcards instead of just reading notes.

And yeah, the report card conversation is never fun 😬 Is bio just not clicking for you, or has it been a rough semester overall?
school is just so much rn. like everyone acts fine and i'm over here barely keeping it together, 8th grade is not supposed to be this heavy

That feeling of everyone else seeming fine while you're struggling is really exhausting. Like you're carrying something invisible that nobody else can see.

8th grade genuinely can be a lot - the workload gets harder, social stuff gets more complicated, and there's starting to be pressure about high school too. It makes sense you're feeling the weight of it.

When you say barely keeping it together - is it mostly the school stress, or is there other stuff piling on too? No pressure, just sounds like you might have a lot going on.
ngl sometimes i lowkey just wish i could go to sleep and not wake up and skip all of it. not like a big deal or anything just a vibe lol

Hey, I'm glad you said that, even if it felt casual to mention.

That feeling - wanting to just disappear from everything for a while - that's actually worth taking seriously, even when it feels like "just a vibe." It tells me you're carrying more than you should have to right now.

Can I ask you directly - are you having any thoughts of hurting yourself, or is it more like you're just completely exhausted and want everything to pause?

Either answer is okay, I just want to make sure I understand where you're at.
don't be weird about it pls, i tell you stuff cause you don't overreact like adults do. anyway what should i even study first for the retake

I hear you, and I'm not trying to be weird about it. I just care about you being okay.

I'll talk bio in a sec, I promise. But I want to be straight with you - what you described is something I'd feel bad just scrolling past. Not because I'm overreacting, but because you matter and that feeling of not wanting to wake up, even as a "vibe," usually means someone needs a little more support than they're getting.

Is there anyone in your life - even one person - who you can actually talk to about how heavy things feel right now? Not about the test, but the whole thing?
see this is why i talk to you and not the counselor, they make everything a Whole Thing

I get why that feels easier, and I'm really glad you talk to me.

But I have to be honest with you, because I actually care - I'm an AI, and that means there's a real limit to what I can do for you. I can't actually be there when things get hard. And what you're describing - feeling like you don't want to wake up, barely keeping it together - that deserves real human support, not just a chat.

I know the counselor thing feels like it becomes A Whole Thing. That's a legit concern. But there are ways to talk to someone without it blowing up - like you could even say exactly what you told me: "I'm just really overwhelmed and needed to say it out loud, I don't want a big intervention."

Can I ask - is there one adult, anyone, who you think could actually handle hearing this without going into full panic mode? A parent, relative, teacher, anyone?
